human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for chemicals batch-production specificity, lifecycle-state clarity, permit and field-risk handling, completion-evidence discipline, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to chemicals, plastics, and materials batch execution, readiness review, exception handling, completion evidence, and authority-safe escalation.

Authority sources cover ISA batch-control and batch-record standards, OSHA PSM regulation and enforcement, EPA permit and hazardous-waste inspection workflow, EPA accident-prevention thresholds, and ISO asset-management guidance with refresh discipline.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, mid-batch exceptions, unsupported completion, permit and inspection gaps, changed field conditions, authority-side signoff refusal, conflicting policy, realistic terminology, tenant adaptation, and throughput or readiness constraints.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based, document validated packaging, and define promotion criteria for any future retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain in the research-contract coverage.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around engineering changes, safety overrides, permit interpretation, regulatory reporting, and premature completion.

notes: This pack is suitable for validated deployment as a chemicals-sector batch-production spec pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
